message:
fix(tui): align table header columns with row border indicators

This commit fixes a subtle visual misalignment in the dashboard's
pane grid where header columns were shifted relative to data rows.

## Problem

The pane grid header columns were not aligned with the data rows
because data rows include a colored left border indicator ("▌") that
the header row didn't account for:

```
# T S Model          Title            <- Header (no border space)
▌1 󰊠 ◐ opus          Main Agent       <- Data row (has border)
▌2 󰘧 ○ o1            Secondary        <- Border pushes content right
```

This created a 1-character offset where:
- The "#" header was above the border indicator, not the index
- All subsequent columns were similarly misaligned

## Solution

Added a placeholder space in the header to account for the border
indicator, and adjusted the Type column width to match:

```go
parts = append(parts, " ") // Border indicator placeholder (matches row's "▌")
parts = append(parts, " ") // Selection column
parts = append(parts, headerStyle.Width(2).Render("#"))
parts = append(parts, headerStyle.Width(1).Render("T")) // Width(1) to match row's icon
```

## Result

```
  # T S Model          Title           <- Header now aligned
▌ 1 󰊠 ◐ opus          Main Agent      <- Data rows match
▌ 2 󰘧 ○ o1            Secondary       <- Perfect column alignment
```

## Technical Details

- Added single space placeholder for border indicator column
- Changed Type column from Width(2) to Width(1) to match the actual
  icon width used in data rows
- Comments added to clarify the alignment relationship

This is a visual polish fix with no behavioral changes.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -793,9 +793,10 @@ func RenderTableHeader(dims LayoutDimensions, t theme.Theme) string {
 		BorderForeground(t.Surface1)
 
 	var parts []string
+	parts = append(parts, " ") // Border indicator placeholder (matches row's "▌")
 	parts = append(parts, " ") // Selection column
 	parts = append(parts, headerStyle.Width(2).Render("#"))
-	parts = append(parts, headerStyle.Width(2).Render("T"))
+	parts = append(parts, headerStyle.Width(1).Render("T")) // Width(1) to match row's icon
 
 	if dims.ShowStatusCol {
 		parts = append(parts, headerStyle.Width(1).Render("S"))
